name: Heqet
description: Heqet is an ancient Egyptian goddess associated with fertility, childbirth, and the life-giving waters of the Nile. She is often depicted as a frog or a woman with a frog's head, symbolizing her connection to creation and new life. Heqet's origins are traced back to the Early Dynastic Period, making her one of the oldest known Egyptian deities. Her primary role was to assist women during childbirth, ensuring a safe delivery and protecting both mother and child. As a goddess of fertility, she was also invoked to promote successful harvests and the abundance of the land. Her association with water further reinforces her role as a source of life and renewal. Heqet was believed to breathe life into newborn babies at the moment of birth, and her presence was considered essential for a successful delivery. She was closely linked to Khnum, the ram-headed god who fashioned humans on his potter's wheel, with Heqet breathing life into the clay forms. Her influence extended beyond childbirth, as she was also venerated as a protector of the deceased, guiding them through the afterlife. Temples dedicated to Heqet were established throughout Egypt, and her image was frequently incorporated into amulets and other protective charms. Her enduring presence in Egyptian mythology highlights the importance of fertility, childbirth, and the life-giving forces of nature in ancient Egyptian society.
subtitle: Goddess of Fertility and Childbirth

keyMyths.title: Heqet and the Breath of Life
keyMyths.description: Heqet breathes life into the clay figures created by Khnum, giving them the essence of life at the moment of creation. This highlights her role in the creation process and her association with bringing life into existence.

keyMyths.title: Heqet's Assistance During Childbirth
keyMyths.description: Heqet is invoked during childbirth to ensure a safe and successful delivery, protecting both the mother and the newborn child from harm. Her presence is considered essential for a positive outcome and the well-being of the family.

keyMyths.title: Heqet's Role in the Afterlife
keyMyths.description: Heqet guides the deceased through the afterlife, offering protection and assistance on their journey to the next world. Her presence ensures a safe passage and a successful transition into the realm of the dead.

sources.author: Geraldine Pinch
sources.work: Egyptian Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Goddesses, and Traditions

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
